paper_title: Factor Analysis and Pollen Data Study in Szeged

methods.description: Factor analysis is used to identify linear relationships among variables, reducing dimensionality without losing substantial information. This method transforms original variables into fewer latent variables (factors) that explain the joint behavior of past and current meteorological elements and pollen concentration. A special transformation is applied to determine the influence of explanatory variables on the resultant variable, ranking their importance.

steps.description: Apply factor analysis to the initial datasets consisting of 9 variables (8 explanatory variables and 1 resultant variable).
steps.searchable_summary: perform factor analysis on dataset with 9 variables

steps.description: Determine the number of retained factors by specifying a least percentage (80%) of the total variance in the original variables.
steps.searchable_summary: determine number of factors based on 80% variance criterion

steps.description: Perform a special transformation of the retained factors to discover the influence of explanatory variables on the resultant variable.
steps.searchable_summary: apply special transformation to retained factors

steps.description: Combine all weights of the factors with the resultant variable into one factor, ensuring only one factor has a significant weight with the resultant variable.
steps.searchable_summary: combine factor weights with resultant variable

steps.description: Perform factor analysis with special transformation for each of the 186 9-dimensional data sets.
steps.searchable_summary: perform factor analysis on 186 data sets

methods.description: This method describes the geographical location and climate of Szeged, Hungary, and details the data collection process for daily pollen counts of 19 taxa over an 11-year period.

steps.description: Collect daily pollen counts of 19 taxa over the 11-year period 1997-2007.
steps.searchable_summary: collect daily pollen counts for 19 taxa from 1997 to 2007

steps.description: Identify the taxa with the highest pollen levels, which include Ambrosia, Poaceae, Populus, and Urtica.
steps.searchable_summary: identify taxa with highest pollen levels
outputs: list of taxa with highest pollen levels
